mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding one percentage point signals an abrupt labor market deterioration that triggers recession risk pricing, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances when the unemployment threshold is breached, or t-stat for return difference is insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously high real sales of new autos and low federal funds rates indicate robust consumer credit expansion and purchasing power, which amplifies corporate revenue growth and produces positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform baseline by >2% annualized, or interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing output and supply chain utilization, which directly supports near-term corporate earnings momentum and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during supply-constrained regimes, or 5-day forward returns show no predictive power when industrial production trends upward.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrates sustained labor market strength that supports aggregate consumption and corporate profit margins, yielding positive equity forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict positive returns in >58% of observations, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.03 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation print exceeding 0.4 percent signals accelerating price pressures that force central banks to tighten policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in >65% of high-inflation threshold breaches, or the inflation threshold shows no incremental predictive power over baseline models.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in housing starts and industrial production indicates synchronized strength across construction and manufacturing sectors, which drives broad-based economic growth and generates positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal underperforms single-series benchmarks by >1.5% annualized, or interaction effects vanish in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real gross domestic product confirms an economic contraction that reduces corporate cash flows and investor risk appetite, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDP contractions fail to predict negative returns in >60% of cases, or the signal loses predictive accuracy when adjusted for lagged market momentum.
